title: [InstCombine] Fix crash when alloc functions are missing `alloc-family`
summary:
The generalization extends the removal of unused allocations beyond standard `malloc`/`free` pairs to support custom allocation families and stack allocations. The patch modifies `isAllocSiteRemovable` to only check for matching allocation families when a `Family` is specified, allowing `alloca` and custom allocators (with no family) to bypass these checks. This enables the optimization to eliminate `alloca` followed by `free` and custom allocator/deallocator pairs, as shown in the generalized test cases.

**Key insight**: The original code assumed all allocations had a family, requiring assertions. The fix makes family matching conditional, gracefully handling cases without one. This pattern is reusable: when generalizing an optimization, identify implicit assumptions (like mandatory family matching) and relax them conditionally, expanding coverage while preserving safety. Always verify with tests for both new and edge cases.
